positive_reviews: NoÃ¶ne warned me that at room temperature this feels mighty cold when first being applied to the lower back. positive_reviews: I could not function without my Icey Hot! I use this and even put the "roll on" over it. I was standing on top of a 3ft desk straightening curtains over three years ago. I stepped back forgetting where I was and fell off. I had three neck surgeries in the past so all I kept saying was protect the neck. My head never touched the floor but my left shoulder, elbow, and hip took the brunt of it! Mostly my shoulder. I seemed okay but was in pain. Waited a few weeks to go see a doctor. He said it was my neck and I had six needle injections. Kept telling them that my shoulder and upper arm hurt and kept me from sleeping. The doctor just did not listen. Went to a new doctor who immediately ordered a MRI. My rotator cuff was badly ripped and I had a bone spur on top of my shoulder. Needed surgery. I had been using only Icey Hot Pro cream and the original roll on Icey Hot. Carried a roll on in my purse. Stomach issues prevent me from taking any anti-inflammatory medicine or aspirin. Slept with ice bags and had my heating pad to help at night. Had the surgery. It was my 13th surgery. They had to use a plastic screw to attach the torn ligament. Never again! I used Icey Hot to help me get through recovery and PT. Well, last March, I ripped my right rotator cuff and once again, Icey Hot Pro and the roll on Icey Hot became my saving grace! Two Orthopedic surgeons say I need surgery. I am trying Stem Cell next week. Just can not go through that surgery again. I am right handed!! I have used Icey Hot, ice and heat to get through. Nights are the worst! I keep Icey Hot by my bed, in my purse, and several places around the house! I can not do without it. Have tried other products through those years but nothing works like the strong Icey Hot. Menthal is my new perfume smell! God bless the Icey Hot inventor and the company! I truly can not live without it!

And I love that it comes in different sizes! Iâve tried prescription lidocaine patches and they were no good and didnât stick on at all. Icy Hot patches do not come off until you take them off hours later or the next day. The only issue is that itâs a strong menthol smell that can burn your eyes if itâs close to your face. Make sure you wash your hands after applying so you donât burn your eyes by touching with hands! I have a constant supply for myself and family in pain. If I could invest in these I would. Iâll be using them for life. Extremely happy customer!!!

Long acting pain relief that comes in 2 sizes. The large size is good for large surfaces (back) and the smaller patches are good for things like shins, calves, achilles. The packages are re-sealable to keep the from drying out once opened. The active ingredient is Menthol 5%, which is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID). Other brands use Lidocaine, which is NOT an NSAID and may not be suitable for everyone. I prefer to use topical aids such as patches as they target the area where relief is needed. Patches stay on the skin for many hours, you can hide them under clothes easily if needed . Creams wear off, rub off on clothing can be greasy/oily and stain. Oral meds take time to travel thru the body before they get to the target area to provide relief. Convenient to carry and easy to apply. Works well and $1.20 per patch is a good value for this combo pack.

I own these in every size! I am never without these patches. I workout with weights and I am often sore and I put the appropriate size on overnight and by morning I am good to go. They do smell like menthol but that just means theyâre working! They stay put until you take them off. I have NEVER had one come off on its own. If I need a small one for my wrist, I just cut it to size and slap it on. The X Large size is perfect for back pain. The medium is great for shoulder or calf soreness. Do yourself a favor and keep these on hand! These are so easy to apply, just peel and stick. They are extremely effective and the heat level is perfect on sore muscles.

Great pads, they've definitely been essential after a serious amount of extra labor, leaving my muscles aching and my back worn. You get ten pads in the box, five medium and five large. They are all individually wrapped and offer a large surface area to cover affected muscles well. After removing the backing and applying, you then enjoy the cooling sensation of the menthol, which helps address dull pains. It is then followed by a warm sensation which also helps relax the affected area and help clear away pain. Especially nice is the rated eight hours of effective performance, which ensures that you never go about your day in pain. Hide them underneath your clothing for excellent relief! Great set, definitely recommended if you suffer from pains.

positive_reviews: I decided to give IcyHot a try on my back after dealing with an injury that just refused to heal. Iâd been using lidocaine patches and gel, which do help, but I was curious about the whole âicyâ and âhotâ thing. And thankfully, it really does what it says. First you get that cool, almost refreshing icy sensation, and then it slowly shifts into a warm, soothing heat. For me, itâs been a solid pain reliever, not a cure, unfortunately, but definitely something that takes the edge off for a few hours. And for that alone, I think itâs worth having on hand. I also love the roll-on applicator. Itâs super convenient and keeps everything mess-free. So yes, Iâd recommend it to anyone who wants some reliable pain relief, as long as youâre not expecting a miracle fix.

I personally have not had any issues with them, but keep in mind, the nerve injury disrupts how much I can feel so I haven't had any problems with that. If you ask me, yes, it is worth it.

positive_reviews: I got these on a whim seeking some quick pain relief for my boyfriend. He had ankle repair surgery and throughout his recovery, his back has become increasingly stiff and âlocks upâ on him. Mind you he already has some bulging discs from a back injury so thereâs just a lot going on. His pain peaked one night and the lidocaine patches heâs prescribed just werenât cutting it. Out of desperation I bought these. He was under the impression BioFreeze and IcyHot were the same thing so I had to convince him to try these. INSTANT AND MAJOR PAIN RELIEF for him wearing this at night. We are so grateful. They alleviate his pain, put him right to sleep, and that he can feel it working for up to 10 hours. We love how large they are in size for extra coverage. We are going to try and get something like this covered by his insurance now. THANK you IcyHot for making such an effective and quality product! Btw, they absolutely stick. If itâs not sticking to your skin that is likely because there is a barrier. Moisturizer, lotion, oil, dirt, etc. try rubbing the area with rubbing alcohol first, let it dry, and I guarantee itâll stick afterwards.

I use these a lot, and they are way better than the ones with lidocaine that cost so much more. They got me through a 15.5 hour flight from LAX to Melbourne when the outlet would not power my heating pad. They got me through Oogie Boogie Bash. And they get me through times when I can't bring my special pillows or heating pad. Just snap as the directions indicate, and they are easy to apply. I find removal can be an issue if they haven't been on very long because they are still quite sticky, but running some water on them helps quite a lot. From my experience, they start working in a few minutes, and they last strongly for about three hours. They are mild for about two more hours, and after that, I don't really notice anything. I have a spinal injury from being rear-ended, and the pain is constant, so I've tried a lot of things in the last 15 years. I also like that these are in a resealable pouch that can easily be tucked in a purse or maybe even a pocket, so you can take them on the go.

May seem pricey at 3 patches per box but for all -day pain relief, these are well worth it. We always keep a box on hand for the 2-3 x year we need them. They are a bit tricky to apply, best to have some help. Can also be cut into smaller sizes, just cut a slit in the top to help with peeling. Make sure you apply to clean and dry skin and smooth it out while applying a bit of pressure to help it adhere better. Hold it in place with a fitted tee tucked under your clothes.

We decided to try IcyHot after seeing the TV commercials (Shaq convinced us!) and comparing it to our usual products (Vick's VapoRub, BioFreeze, and Asian green medicine). We're very impressed! â First Impressions & Sensation âThe relief comes on quickly and definitely delivers on its name: âInitial Feel: It starts off with a strong, pleasant cooling sensation (the "Icy"). âThe Shift: It transitions relatively quickly to a deep, comforting warmth (the "Hot"). âDuration: We found that the warming sensation lasts quite a while, providing sustained relief. âScent and Future Use âI personally love the menthol smellâit's strong, familiar, and feels therapeutic. I'm looking forward to applying this to my back, quads, and knees after intense pickleball sessions. âFinal Verdict âThis is a fantastic pain relief ointment. We would highly recommend it, especially when you can find it available on sale!

positive_reviews: FANTASTIC! Where has this been all my life? LOL While 1.25 ounces may seem small up to other Icy Hot products, the balm easily lasts as long as the 2 ounce Icy Hot Advanced cream and almost as long as the 3 ounce Icy Hot Pro cream with the no-mess applicator. Like the Icy Hot cream roll-on that has the same 16% menthol and 11% camphor, this balm also works within 10 minutes or so, but unlike the cream, you don't have as much of a chance of putting too much on and getting a chemical burn (I cut the cream Â½ and Â½ with a lidocaine cream and it works well, but putting too much of the Icy Hot Advanced cream *without* cutting it with something else that doesn't burn can lead to redness and burning.) You don't have to be worried about this Icy Hot Pro balm/sphere, because you can't put too much on. It *does* work, and works as quickly as pretty much anything else on the market - actually faster than many things like menthol alone, menthol and lidocaine, and/or capsaicin products (chili pepper oil creame.) If you put another layer on before it kicks in, you still will not get burned. This allows you to use the balm as a large roller so you can massage your muscles, pushing the medicine in your skin and also loosen up the tension. Only improvement I can see is that the ring to put your fingers through could be larger - possibly with two holes to be able to use one or two fingers to hold the ball. My hands are not small, but they aren't huge either. My size 9 fingers are too tight to out three fingers in the handle, which would make the handle easier to maneuver. With only two fingers in the handle, the handle is a little loose. Maybe the handle could be larger, with one side being big enough for two fingers and the other side being only for one finger. This would allow for one finger, two fingers, or three fingers (two on one side and one on the other side at the same time) to control the gliding action. If you enlarge the handle, I suppose the whole globe would be larger - maybe it could be a 2 ounce large size (keeping this 1.25 ounce size as well.) I'd buy both! This would work well as a push-up small round deodorant stick type balm too (if Icy Hot is listening!) A long and slender glide on balm would allow for a little more reach, to hit places on my back that I cannot reach with this size. Still, after 20+ years of horrific pain with rheumatoid arthritis and ankylosing spondylitis, constantly shifting creams, ointments, and liquid roll-ons, this is AMAZING! If I have to pack only one thing for pain (especially if it has to be something I can use in public, making the creams and ointments less easy to maneuver,) *THIS* is what I pack every time!

positive_reviews: Personal Use: Having suffered from neck, shoulder, mid and lower back pain, I used to have used these patches almost everywhere. I also recently sprained my thumb and trimmed on of these patches and wrapped it around my thumb and palm. These are awesome for bursitis. tendonitis, strains, sprains, and any muscle tenderness or tightness. I use these several times a week, mainly on my neck/shoulders, due to tightness from sitting in front a of a pc for 10 hrs a day. I can wear them under clothes to work, or under PJs at night. And they last anywhere from 8-12 hours and stay in place. They work amazingly and consistently. I would probably be popping pain meds weekly if it werent for these life-savers. Downside(if any): Unless youve had the patch on for 8-10 hours, do not shower with hot water for an hour after wearing these. Your skin will feel like its on fire. Same with sweating. If you work out after work, take it off after lunch. If you work out in the morning, give yourself an hour before you work out. Also, they are super sticky. If you leave it on for at least 4 -5 hours,. they are easier to take off. Regardless of these two things, which i consider minor because of how great they work, i wouldnt trade them for anything and will continue to use 3-5 a week. Ingredients: Active ingredient is Menthol 5% Price: Amazon has the best price I have found so far, and becomes even better when you get on Sub&Save. Highly recommend!

I really like this Icy Hot Max Strength cream and find it to be a very practical addition to my recovery routine. The first major benefit is the fast-acting dual formula, which uses both lidocaine and menthol to quickly desensitize nerves and provide a cooling sensation that cuts through muscle and joint pain in minutes. Second, I appreciate the non-greasy texture of the cream; it absorbs well into the skin without leaving a messy residue, so I can apply it and get on with my day or head to bed without worrying about ruining my clothes or sheets. It is a high-quality topical that consistently delivers on its promises.

I bought this Icy Hot Advanced Pro Pain Cream for my mom after she mentioned missing the old Icy Hot sponge applicator (which has been discontinued). I was hoping this would be a close alternative, and she said it really is. What she likes most is that it allows for a more hands-free type of application, which is important for her since she doesnât enjoy using her hands to rub ointments directly onto sore areas. It still provides that familiar cooling and warming sensation Icy Hot is known for, and it seems to offer good temporary relief. The only reason for 4 stars instead of 5 is personal preferenceâshe wishes it came in a gel form rather than a cream. A gel would feel a bit cleaner and closer to what she used before. Overall, itâs a solid pain relief option and a good replacement for the discontinued applicator-style product.

I tried the plastic spray bottle, the rollerball "stick", & this aerosol/continuous spray. The roller applicator & this aerosol spray came in the "Extra / Maximum strength". The Extra / Max pain formulas may bring long-term relief from added ingredients. 1 - Best (my fave) is the rollerball This treatment felt like the most potent application. The rollerball let's you press hard on the tight spots, the pressure can help loosen painful areas. Make sure you can reach the spot by hand, if you will be applying the roller style option on yourself! The sprays all disperse & reach quite well! 2 - Aerosol - Extra Strength Continuous Pain Relief Spray 4 Ounce Mess Free - has the added ingredients, and is superbly easy to apply. Might get more waste or atomized overspray, but this lays down a nice coating of quick relief! 3 - Spray bottle - another 5 STAR product. Quick relief - large size option. Menthol mostly is the active ingredient in the regular strength products. Ratings: all 3 options = 5 star reviews! More accurately; 5.0 stars for the roller application - 4.75 stars for this featured Aerosol / Continuous spray can - 4.5 ish for the plastic pump spray bottle. I suggest you get a rollerball "stick" & the aerosol spray can, or spray bottle. They reach & apply differently. I apply these with an exhaust fan running or outside. The dry coating is MILD smelling - not as bad as others, but it is a menthol-esque smell for sensitive noses or anyone with disdain for smells from pain topicals.

positive_reviews: Theyâre a lifesaver and a nice addition to anyoneâs first aid. Be mindful of Lidocaine patches, while they are effective, they can cause further skin irritation with uneven use. Youâll need to read the directions for these as they are but a percentage point lower than prescription lidocaine patches, and depending on the individual, your symptoms may be less than worthy of using these for long term use. But for me, I had upper back pain in a particular area and they work well for about 3 hours of use, then kinda fade out over the day. Youâre not supposed to use more than 3-4 a day, so iâd go along with that. If after a few hours pain is returning, or itâs in a different area, itâs fine to take the one you have off and cover the prior area and new area, itâs all dependent on how painful it is, for me I was experiencing a numbness and itchiness in that part of my back. The icyhot formula has always worked, but lidocaine is a perfect addition to their standard formula. Iâm satisfied to say the least, and a little peeling doesnât bother me I assure you when the pain is relieved. But with that said, the adhesive is abysmally bad. They really need to work on that. I advise that you take as many fabrics as you can off that may collide with the product. You can also use a little surgical tape to help keep it on if you have some handy. Otherwise, keep pressing.

positive_reviews: FANTASTIC! Where has this been all my life? LOL While 1.25 ounces may seem small up to other Icy Hot products, the balm easily lasts as long as the 2 ounce Icy Hot Advanced cream and almost as long as the 3 ounce Icy Hot Pro cream with the no-mess applicator. Like the Icy Hot cream roll-on that has the same 16% menthol and 11% camphor, this balm also works within 10 minutes or so, but unlike the cream, you don't have as much of a chance of putting too much on and getting a chemical burn (I cut the cream Â½ and Â½ with a lidocaine cream and it works well, but putting too much of the Icy Hot Advanced cream *without* cutting it with something else that doesn't burn can lead to redness and burning.) You don't have to be worried about this Icy Hot Pro balm/sphere, because you can't put too much on. It *does* work, and works as quickly as pretty much anything else on the market - actually faster than many things like menthol alone, menthol and lidocaine, and/or capsaicin products (chili pepper oil creame.) If you put another layer on before it kicks in, you still will not get burned. This allows you to use the balm as a large roller so you can massage your muscles, pushing the medicine in your skin and also loosen up the tension. Only improvement I can see is that the ring to put your fingers through could be larger - possibly with two holes to be able to use one or two fingers to hold the ball. My hands are not small, but they aren't huge either. My size 9 fingers are too tight to out three fingers in the handle, which would make the handle easier to maneuver. With only two fingers in the handle, the handle is a little loose. Maybe the handle could be larger, with one side being big enough for two fingers and the other side being only for one finger. This would allow for one finger, two fingers, or three fingers (two on one side and one on the other side at the same time) to control the gliding action. If you enlarge the handle, I suppose the whole globe would be larger - maybe it could be a 2 ounce large size (keeping this 1.25 ounce size as well.) I'd buy both! This would work well as a push-up small round deodorant stick type balm too (if Icy Hot is listening!) A long and slender glide on balm would allow for a little more reach, to hit places on my back that I cannot reach with this size. Still, after 20+ years of horrific pain with rheumatoid arthritis and ankylosing spondylitis, constantly shifting creams, ointments, and liquid roll-ons, this is AMAZING! If I have to pack only one thing for pain (especially if it has to be something I can use in public, making the creams and ointments less easy to maneuver,) *THIS* is what I pack every time!

Personal Use: Having suffered from neck, shoulder, mid and lower back pain, I used to have used these patches almost everywhere. I also recently sprained my thumb and trimmed on of these patches and wrapped it around my thumb and palm. These are awesome for bursitis. tendonitis, strains, sprains, and any muscle tenderness or tightness. I use these several times a week, mainly on my neck/shoulders, due to tightness from sitting in front a of a pc for 10 hrs a day. I can wear them under clothes to work, or under PJs at night. And they last anywhere from 8-12 hours and stay in place. They work amazingly and consistently. I would probably be popping pain meds weekly if it werent for these life-savers. Downside(if any): Unless youve had the patch on for 8-10 hours, do not shower with hot water for an hour after wearing these. Your skin will feel like its on fire. Same with sweating. If you work out after work, take it off after lunch. If you work out in the morning, give yourself an hour before you work out. Also, they are super sticky. If you leave it on for at least 4 -5 hours,. they are easier to take off. Regardless of these two things, which i consider minor because of how great they work, i wouldnt trade them for anything and will continue to use 3-5 a week. Ingredients: Active ingredient is Menthol 5% Price: Amazon has the best price I have found so far, and becomes even better when you get on Sub&Save. Highly recommend!

Arrived on time and as described. The small jar of Icy Hot Balm feels a bit greasy at first and a bit like hard wax. I was hesitant to use it at first, but at the insistence of my therapist I went forward. It has a strong menthol scent similar to "Vick's", which I don't mind (but my cat does). As soon as you apply it to your skin the waxy feel melts and penetrates easily into your skin and the greasy feeling can be rubbed in as well, although it does remain to a point. I would advise covering wherever you apply it if you worry about the Icy Hot transferring onto other fabrics, furniture, bedding, etc. To be fair, I'm not sure if it takes away the pain or if the sensation of the menthol distracts one from the pain for a few hours, either way it does help ease minor muscle ache in my experience. I had a full knee joint replacement and wasn't able to tolerate the Rx meds so I was relying on OTC Tylenol and the Icy Hot to keep me "happy". The pain *I* was most experiencing was calf muscle pain, and pain around my ankle, both of which were helped with elevation, icing therapy, and the Icy Hot massaged into the pain areas (avoiding the incision). After a while I was only using the Icy Hot, particularly at night so I could sleep. Bottom Line: For safe OTC minor to mild muscle pain relief that lasts a few hours before reapplication, I would recommend.

Iâll be using them for life. Extremely happy customer!!!
negative_reviews: I had been working in the garage on some shelving, and went to bed very sore. I woke up at 3 a.m. and found a new bottle of Icy Hot No Mess...I've used both BioFreeze and Icy Hot in the past with no problem. I put it on my forearms and the backs of my hands and went back to bed. About an hour later, I woke up in EXCRUCIATING pain. It was as if I had used capsaicin, but Icy Hot NEVER has capsaicin in it, which is why I buy their product. I checked the label, and it said the "active ingredient" was menthol. My arms were burning, but eventually I went back to sleep. That morning I woke up and my arms and hands burned even worse. When I started working in the driveway in the sun (about 85Â°F out), I felt like I was getting a VERY SEVERE SUNBURN. I went into the shade, but it still burned. I found the bottle and checked again, and sure enough, there is CAPSAICIN in the FREAKIN' INACTIVE INGREDIENT LIST!!! And it says you might feel a "slight" burning sensation that will go away IN A FEW DAYS??? I hate you people! I literally had to stop working on my project for TWO DAYS because I couldn't stand the excruciating burning pain! Icy Hot, if I wanted a capsaicin product, I would have purchased another brand with capsaicin! I will NEVER trust your products again, and I'm going back to BioFreeze. Buyer BEWARE.

So this week I had a really tight glute and figured I would try an icy-hot patch since it stays local to where you place the patch, avoiding accidentally getting some in my groin. Unfortunately the Icy Hot patch is WEAK AF! I almost feel like I got scammed because they're so ineffective. It barely feels like it's doing anything. I'll be sticking to my usual peppermint oil (Now foods Organic brand). It's easily 20-30 times stronger and delivers superior results. I've used many brands of peppermint oil and the Now Foods brand and Doterra are the strongest. All others either feel diluted or they didn't harvest the leaves at the correct time of day and season. Stick to Now Foods Organic and you'll be amazed. I wont be buying Icy Hot products anymore.
